question_text: Evaluate the claim that 'a 100 W light bulb always uses more energy than a 60 W light bulb.' Use scientific reasoning and consider different scenarios to support your evaluation.
correct_answer: The claim is not always true. While a 100 W bulb has a higher power rating, total energy consumed depends on both power and time of operation. A 100 W bulb used for 1 hour consumes 360,000 J, but a 60 W bulb used for 2 hours consumes 432,000 J, so the lower-power bulb uses more total energy.
explanation: Energy is calculated using E = P × t. A higher power rating doesn't automatically mean more total energy is used—it depends on how long the device runs. A 60 W bulb running for longer can use more total energy than a 100 W bulb running for a shorter time. This question requires students to think beyond just power ratings and consider the full picture.
